slide_title: Longest Prefix Matching (LPM) - Need and Importance
content:
LPM is required in IP routing to determine the most specific route for a given destination IP address.

Key benefits of LPM include:
• Ensures accurate forwarding decisions
• Allows route aggregation and hierarchical routing
• Supports overlapping IP prefixes
• Improves scalability of routing tables
• Enables fine-grained control over traffic routing

slide_title: Binary Search Tree (BST) IP Lookup Implementation
content:
Key Points:
• Routing table entries are stored as nodes in a BST, ordered by IP prefix values.
• The algorithm works by comparing the destination IP with node prefixes, traversing the tree, and tracking the best match.
• Time complexity: Average case O(log N), Worst case O(N) due to unbalanced tree.

slide_title: Working of Binary Search Tree IP Lookup
content:
Key Steps:
• Convert IP addresses to binary or integer form
• Insert prefixes into BST based on numeric order
• Traverse BST while checking prefix matches
• Track the best (longest) match

slide_title: Advantages of Binary Search Tree IP Lookup
content:
Key Points:
• Simple implementation
• Easier to understand
• Fast average case performance (O(log N))

slide_title: Disadvantages of Binary Search Tree IP Lookup
content:
Key Points:
• Not optimized for prefix matching
• Performance degrades with large routing tables
• Requires extra checks for LPM
A Binary Trie is a tree data structure optimized for prefix matching, where each node represents a single bit (0 or 1).
